title: Hobbies & Leisure
shortSummary: Talk about what you enjoy doing in your free time — sports, entertainment, music, and social activities.
culturalNote: Football (fútbol) is by far the most popular sport in all Spanish-speaking countries — it's not just a sport but a cultural identity. Going to the cinema is affordable and popular, with many films shown in their original language with Spanish subtitles (versión original subtitulada, V.O.S.). Socialising centres around food and drink — the 'tertulia' (informal intellectual gathering at a cafe) has a long tradition in Spanish culture.

keyPhrases.spanish: En mi tiempo libre leo libros
keyPhrases.english: In my free time I read books
keyPhrases.pronunciation: ehn mee tee-EHM-poh LEE-breh LEH-oh LEE-brohs

keyPhrases.english: Do you want to go to the cinema tonight?
keyPhrases.pronunciation: kee-EH-rehs eer ahl SEE-neh EHS-tah NOH-cheh

keyPhrases.english: I've played guitar since I was a child

keyPhrases.spanish: No me gusta nada el fútbol
keyPhrases.english: I don't like football at all
keyPhrases.pronunciation: noh meh GOOS-tah NAH-dah ehl FOOT-bohl

keyPhrases.spanish: Los fines de semana salgo con amigos
keyPhrases.english: On weekends I go out with friends
keyPhrases.pronunciation: lohs FEE-nehs deh seh-MAH-nah SAHL-goh kohn ah-MEE-gohs
keyPhrases.context: Salir = to go out (irregular: yo salgo)

keyPhrases.english: I prefer to stay at home

keyPhrases.context: 'Dar un paseo' = to go for a walk/stroll

keyPhrases.english: What type of music do you like?
keyPhrases.pronunciation: keh TEE-poh deh MOO-see-kah teh GOOS-tah

grammar.explanation: 'Gustar' works backwards from English: the thing you like is the subject. Use 'gusta' + singular noun or infinitive, 'gustan' + plural noun. The person who likes uses an indirect object pronoun: me, te, le, nos, os, les.
grammar.commonMistake: Saying 'yo gusto el cine' instead of 'me gusta el cine' — gustar doesn't work like English 'like'.

grammar.rule: Jugar a vs Tocar: playing sports vs instruments
grammar.explanation: 'Jugar a' is for sports and games: jugar al fútbol, jugar al tenis. 'Tocar' is for musical instruments: tocar la guitarra, tocar el piano. Never mix them up.

examples.en: She plays the piano very well

grammar.commonMistake: Saying 'juego la guitarra' — instruments use 'tocar', not 'jugar'.

grammar.explanation: Several common verbs follow the same backwards structure as gustar: encantar (to love), interesar (to interest), molestar (to bother), importar (to matter), parecer (to seem). They all use me/te/le + verb.

examples.en: I don't care / It doesn't matter to me

grammar.commonMistake: Treating these like normal verbs: 'yo encanto viajar' should be 'me encanta viajar'.

dialogue.setup: Two new friends, Sofía and James, discover they share some hobbies.

lines.speaker: Sofía
lines.english: What do you do in your free time?

lines.speaker: James
lines.spanish: Me gusta mucho el deporte. Juego al fútbol y hago surf.
lines.english: I really like sport. I play football and surf.

lines.speaker: Sofía
lines.spanish: ¡A mí también me encanta el surf! ¿Dónde surfeas?
lines.english: I love surfing too! Where do you surf?

lines.speaker: James
lines.spanish: En la playa de Bondi, normalmente. ¿Y tú, qué hobbies tienes?
lines.english: At Bondi Beach, usually. And you, what hobbies do you have?

lines.speaker: Sofía
lines.spanish: Me gusta leer y toco la guitarra. También me encanta cocinar.
lines.english: I like reading and I play the guitar. I also love cooking.

lines.speaker: James
lines.spanish: ¿Quieres ir al cine este fin de semana?
lines.english: Do you want to go to the cinema this weekend?

lines.speaker: Sofía
lines.english: Yes! What type of films do you like?

lines.speaker: James
lines.spanish: Me gustan las de acción, pero no me gustan nada las de terror.
lines.english: I like action films, but I don't like horror films at all.

quiz.question: How do you say 'I like sports' in Spanish?
How do you say 'I play the guitar'?
quiz.explanation: 'Tocar' = to play an instrument. 'Jugar' = to play a sport/game.

quiz.question: How do you say 'Do you want to go to the cinema?'
quiz.explanation: Querer + infinitive: 'quieres ir' = you want to go.

quiz.question: Which is correct: 'Me gusta los libros' or 'Me gustan los libros'?
quiz.explanation: 'Libros' is plural, so the verb must be 'gustan' (plural).
